DP.041.0000:
[0103,0001]: Hey, [0103,0000]!
Did you come to study, too?\f
I went ahead and memorized everything
that was up on the blackboard.\f
After all, it’s the Trainer’s job to
avoid having their precious Pokémon\r
hurt in battle, right?\f
So, [0103,0000], what brings you?
Huh?\r
You’ve got something for me?\f
DP.041.0002:
[0103,0001]: So, what is this...?\f
Score!
It’s a Town Map!\f
Huh?!
Why are there two in here?\f
I like it a lot, but I don’t need two.
Here, [0103,0000], you take one!\f
DP.041.0003:
[0103,0001]: Hmm...\f
Well, according to the Town Map,
I guess Oreburgh City is where\r
I should be going next.\f
There’s a Gym, so it’d be perfect for
raising the Pokémon I just caught.\f
Well, I’m on the road to becoming
the greatest Trainer of all time!\f
See you around!\f
DP.041.0004:
You just became a Trainer, did you?\f
You should look up topics that you’re
not familiar with.
DP.041.0006:
We learned about Pokémon types
today. Umm...\f
Grass is weak to Fire,
Fire is weak to Water, and\r
Water is weak to Grass!\f
I think that’s how it goes!
DP.041.0007:
Pokémon are smart enough to use
items they’re holding.\f
But they won’t know what to do with
man-made items like Potions and\r
Antidotes.
DP.041.0008:
I wrote an essay in my notebook on 
what makes a true Pokémon Trainer.
DP.041.0009:
Doing it is the best way to learn!
Can we get a battle with you?
DP.041.0011:
Aww! But battling is the best way
to learn about Pokémon!
DP.041.0012:
It’s hard to win if you don’t have
some Pokémon to choose from.
DP.041.0013:
Whew! You’re really good!\f
We’ve been studying every day, but
we were no match at all!\f
You’d be able to use this Technical
Machine properly. Here you go!\f
DP.041.0014:
A Technical Machine, or TM for short,
contains a Pokémon move.\f
Using a TM, you can instantly teach
its move to a Pokémon.\f
But remember--a TM is good for one
use only. Don’t waste it!
DP.041.0015:
I just became friends with my
Pokémon.\f
I’m not sure if we can do this
properly, but may we battle?
DP.041.0016:
I need to try the things I learned
before I forget them!\f
DP.041.0017:
Boo!
Where’s your sense of adventure?\f
Don’t be shy about talking to people
and getting into battles!
DP.041.0018:
Do you think we would have done
better if I used Potions?
DP.041.0019:
We were actually confident that we
would do pretty good.\f
But I’m completely impressed by how
tough you are.\f
If you’d like, talk to my friend and
get a Technical Machine from him.
DP.041.0020:
The blackboard lists status changes
that may affect Pokémon in battle.\f
DP.041.0021: Which would you like to read about?
DP.041.0022:
If a Pokémon is poisoned, it will
steadily lose HP.\f
The effects of poison remain after
a battle.\f
A poisoned Pokémon’s HP will drop
while it is traveling.\f
Heal a poisoning using an Antidote.\f
DP.041.0023:
If a Pokémon becomes paralyzed,
its Speed stat drops.\f
In addition, it may not be able to
move while it is in battle.\f
Paralysis remains after battle.
Cure it using a Parlyz Heal.\f
DP.041.0024:
If a Pokémon falls asleep, it will be
unable to attack.\f
The Pokémon may wake up on its own,
but if a battle ends while it is\r
sleeping, it will stay asleep.\f
Wake it up using an Awakening.\f
DP.041.0025:
A burn reduces the Attack stat and
steadily reduces the victim’s HP.\f
A burn lingers after battle.
Cure a burn using a Burn Heal.\f
DP.041.0026:
If a Pokémon is frozen, it becomes
completely helpless.\f
The Pokémon may thaw out on its own,
but if a battle ends while it is\r
frozen, it will stay frozen.\f
Thaw it out using an Ice Heal.\f
DP.041.0027:
It’s a notebook filled with writing.
Let’s see...\f
Pokémon are to be caught using
Poké Balls.\f
Up to six Pokémon can accompany
a Trainer.\f
A Trainer is someone who catches
Pokémon, raises them, and battles\r
with them.\f
A Trainer’s mission is to defeat the
strong Trainers who await challengers\r
in Pokémon Gyms.
